title: The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao
author: Junot Díaz

setting.note: The Dominican half of a story that runs between New Jersey and the island. Díaz sets the family's fortunes against the long shadow of the Trujillo dictatorship: the walled Zona Colonial, the seafront Malecón, a quiet capital neighbourhood, the cane fields inland where the family curse turns violent, and Baní to the west, where the line begins. The map anchors on Santo Domingo; Oscar's Paterson sits outside it, to the north.

palette.note: The red of Caribbean heat and the fukú, the family curse that stalks the book. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
